area_description: Memory in LLMs addresses the challenge of maintaining and utilizing information across interactions, and helps users recall past multi-modal memories. Research spans two key aspects: memory recall (retrieving from past interactions and logs to answer recall questions) and memory organization (determining the best way to organize past memory for recall QA and personalized conversation).

categories.name: Memory Recall
categories.description: Retrieving from past interactions and logs to answer users' recall questions, addressing challenges of answer generation across different memories.

sub_topics.name: Sparse Memory QA
sub_topics.description: Recall over fairly sparse past memory, where generating answers across different memories can still be challenging despite low volume.

sub_topics.name: Dense Memory QA
sub_topics.description: Recall over dense past memory with high similarity and many duplicates, making retrieval and noise reduction even more challenging.

categories.name: Memory Organization
categories.description: Determining the best way to organize past memory for recall QA and personalized conversation, spanning linear, layered, graph-based, and internalization approaches.

sub_topics.name: Linear Memory
sub_topics.description: Organizing memory linearly with focus on when to discard and update memory, potentially leveraging RL to maintain a clean and compact memory.

sub_topics.name: Layered Memory
sub_topics.description: Multi-layer memory organization such as core (profile), semantic (factual), and episodic (time/location-associated) layers, with retrieval granularity at session-level, turn-level, and topic-level.

sub_topics.name: Tree/Graph-based Memory
sub_topics.description: Memory structures that mimic human associative memory using trees or graphs, enabling hopping from one memory to another related by topic, location, event, or person.

sub_topics.name: Memory Internalization
sub_topics.description: Storing personal memories into model parameters through techniques such as LoRA, embedding feeding, and other parameter-based approaches.

theme.name: Personalization
theme.description: Papers focused on leveraging memory for personalized conversations, user modeling, and adapting responses based on user history and preferences.

theme.name: Multi-modal Memory
theme.description: Papers addressing memory that spans multiple modalities including text, images, audio, and video from past interactions.

theme.name: Long-term Memory
theme.description: Papers focused on maintaining and utilizing information over extended periods and many interactions, addressing challenges of long-horizon memory persistence.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics for memory systems.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply memory techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art in memory, including taxonomy, advantages, and limitations.
